title: A Tough Decade For Real Estate Stocks
text:
A Tough Decade For Real Estate Stocks

Louis-Vincent Gave

Gavekal Research | 19 Jun 2026

It has been a tough decade for real estate stocks, with the global MSCI real estate index underperforming the MSCI World index by more than 50%. In a sense, this is surprising. A decade ago, money was pouring into real estate funds on the premise that interest rates would stay low for a very long time, making real estate a natural beneficiary. For a while, this held true. But Covid undercut the investment case for real estate in two ways. First, the work-from-home/shop-from-home trend crushed commercial real estate. Second, the fiscal and monetary policy response to Covid planted the seeds for inflation and the consequent interest rate increases.
